donors: Japan - Ministry of Foreign Affairs HAD
Japan - Ministry of Foreign Affairs EGA
Japan - Ministry of Foreign Affairs Year-end Supplementary Budget
Japan - Secretariat of Peace Cooperation HQs
Japan - Grant assistance through UN / International Organization

instructions: Strictly base all content on the provided inputs and knowledge base.
Do not invent or hallucinate facts, figures, dates, precedents, partnerships, or staff details.
If information is missing, explicitly state that it is not available in the context.
All information is to be collected on a non-committal basis.
Focus on projects suitable for predictable earmarked funding.

The Mission of Japan will screen candidate projects based on diplomatic significance, benefits to the Japanese economy or companies, economic security importance, and past project management performance.
Because Japan follows a single-year budgeting system, request only the portion required for FY2027; for multi-year projects, provide a fiscal-year breakdown for subsequent years.
All UNHCR project proposals must be reviewed and cleared by the Donor Relations and Resource Mobilization Service (DRRM) at UNHCR Headquarters.
Projects must commence no later than March 2028.
Project descriptions should be as specific as possible and include quantitative data where available.
Performance targets should be quantitative and verifiable post-implementation through the PDCA cycle.
Provide as much budget detail as possible.
Do not include the JSB-specific elements of urgency, obligatoriness, unpredictability, or non-substitutability.
Use a flat section structure only: do not use subsections, items, or suggested bullet lists. Any required category labels or prompts must be written directly inside the section instructions.
When referencing donor, use donor name and never donor_id.
When referencing outcomes, use outcome name and never outcome_id.
When referencing field context, use field_context name and never field_context_id.

sections.instructions: Provide a compact opening snapshot aligned with the Government of Japan Regular Budget Concept Note front table, capturing country, project title, requested budget for FY2027, sector(s), implementation period, number and breakdown of beneficiaries where available, and explicit reference to contributions to Japan’s policy priorities such as SDGs, Human Security, and the Humanitarian-Development-Peace Nexus. If beneficiary disaggregation or policy alignment details are not available, state this explicitly.

columns.name: Field Context
columns.instructions: Label or category corresponding to the snapshot field.

columns.name: Description
columns.instructions: Concise description aligned with the Government of Japan concept note template.

rows.instructions: Country or countries where the project will be implemented.
Clear project title reflecting key activities, target population, and location.
Amount requested for FY2027 only, in USD.
Relevant sector or sectors such as Health, WASH, Education, Protection, Food, Digital, or other applicable categories.
Total planned implementation duration, indicating start and end months and years.
Total number of beneficiaries with disaggregation by population group where available; if not available, state this explicitly.

Reference applicable SDG numbers, Human Security, Humanitarian-Development-Peace Nexus, or other relevant policy priorities of the Government of Japan; if not applicable or not available, state this explicitly.

sections.instructions: Summarize the operational or crisis context, the most recent identified needs, and the target population using quantified information where available. Clearly state the single overarching objective of the project within the narrative and explain why the proposed response is required at this time. Include the planned implementation period in concise form. Indicate in narrative form whether the project contributes to any of the following categories and briefly justify each that applies: food assistance, health, Green Transformation (GX), Digital Transformation (DX), Women Peace and Security (WPS), regional integration or connectivity, youth, public-private partnership (PPP), rule of law, and relevant Sustainable Development Goals by number. If none apply or information is not available, state this explicitly. Open with quantified context, highlight the most vulnerable groups, and end with a clear statement of what the project will deliver.

Describe the proposed activities in a single coherent narrative, aligned with the Government of Japan concept note requirements. Include quantitative details where available and address, within the same narrative, the strategic or diplomatic significance of the project, any partnerships with Japanese companies or NGOs, coordination with MOFA- or JICA-implemented initiatives, relevance to TICAD, contribution to the Humanitarian-Development Nexus, and inclusion of verifiable performance targets under the PDCA cycle. If any of these elements are not applicable or information is not available, state this explicitly.

Present the expected outcomes and main outputs in a structured narrative without using subsections. Embed quantitative, verifiable targets directly in the text, such as numbers of beneficiaries reached, services delivered, or outputs produced, and explain briefly how these will be verified post-implementation through the PDCA cycle. Indicate within the narrative whether the project aligns with any of Japan’s 17 strategic fields and explain the relevance; if none apply or information is not available, state this explicitly. The strategic fields are AI or semiconductors, digital or cybersecurity, ICT, quantum, defense industry, aerospace, maritime, shipbuilding, materials including critical minerals, synthetic biology or bio, drug discovery or advanced medical care, resource and energy security or GX, fusion energy, foodtech, disaster prevention or national resilience, port logistics, and content.

Explain why the project is strategically relevant and why support from Japan adds value. Within one coherent section, address the response gap being filled, the humanitarian and protection rationale, and the benefits to Japan, including any supported relevance to Japanese diplomatic priorities, human security, the Humanitarian-Development-Peace Nexus, Women, Peace and Security, benefits to Japanese companies or the Japanese economy, and economic security considerations. If the project contributes to supporting Japanese personnel, include the required staff details and expected impact; if not applicable or information is not available, state this explicitly.
State the planned implementation period clearly and confirm that project implementation will commence no later than March 2028.

Provide a detailed budget table showing requested amounts only with breakdown per high level activities. Include one row per high level activity by duplicating the Activity rows as needed. Ensure that indirect or support costs do not exceed 6.5% of the total requested budget and that evaluation has its own separate budget line.

columns.name: Budget Category
columns.instructions: Major cost component per activity grouping. Include activity lines plus evaluation and indirect support costs.

rows.instructions: Direct program costs for the first high level activity.
Direct program costs for the second high level activity; duplicate or add additional Activity rows as needed.
Costs for evaluation activities, clearly separated as its own line.
Indirect Support costs, not exceeding 6.5% of the total requested budget.
Total requested budget (sum of all lines).
